behavior_summary: U8P_TORAX::look is a simple inspection handler for the NPC/object Torax. When the player 'looks at' Torax the script emits a single short bark/text line "torax " and then yields/suspends the process.
There are no menu interactions, branches, flag reads/writes, helper calls, or external function calls; the behavior is purely a descriptive bark followed by process suspend.
The visible bark includes a trailing space in the asset text; this is preserved from the disassembly but has no observable branching effect in this script.
notable_control_flow: Entry -> emit bark "torax " -> suspend/yield. No conditional branches or further actions.
The function executes as a process (processType 0x0208) and exits by suspending rather than modifying game state.
open_questions: Does the trailing space in the bark string carry any gameplay meaning or is it an authoring/asset artifact? (inferential)
Are there any other event hooks or scripts that extend Torax's behavior (dialogue or reactions) elsewhere in the game data that would run in response to other events?

main_function.name: U8P_TORAX::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to player 'U8P_TORAX::look' / inspect action for Torax by displaying a short descriptive bark and then suspending the process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_TORAX::look/inspect callback for the actor (processType 0x0208).
Invoked when the player inspects or uses a 'U8P_TORAX::look' action on the Torax actor/object.

function_map.name: U8P_TORAX::look
function_map.role: Look/inspection handler that emits a single bark and suspends; no side effects or external calls.
